column1: # OWASP ZAP baseline scan rules for vaultctl.
# Format: <rule_id>\t<action>\t<notes>   (tab-separated; lines starting with # are comments)
# Wired into .github/workflows/zap.yml via the action's rules_file_name input.
# When adding a new suppression, link to the commit, PR, or external advisory
# that justifies it. The next person to triage a ZAP issue will want to know
# These are REQUEST headers a browser sends to the server, not RESPONSE
# headers the server is supposed to emit. ZAP's baseline rule misclassifies
# them as missing response headers because a curl/ZAP probe doesn't send
# them. There is no server-side fix; flagging them is noise.

column3: Sec-Fetch-* are request headers, not response headers

column1: # Informational rule. Fires on /api/v1/health because that endpoint is a
# public liveness probe with no sensitive content and no Cache-Control
# header; downstream caching is intentional. Sensitive endpoints already
# return Cache-Control: no-cache via the SecurityHeaders middleware.

column3: /api/v1/health is a public liveness probe; cacheability is intentional
